# Dialect stub — Modern Standard Arabic (formal)

**Scope:** Pan-Arab official copy, some B2B headlines, educational/legal-adjacent summaries — **not a substitute for counsel**.

## When to use

- Government-adjacent services, bank disclaimers (marketing layer only), pan-Arab press releases.
- When brief demands **neutral register** across dialects.

## Guardrails

- MSA can feel cold on consumer social — pair with channel strategy (`seo`, `content`).
- Never invent binding clauses; point to approved Arabic legal text.

## Escalation

Contracts, policies, court-facing language → **qualified legal translator / counsel**.
